item.title: Student Killed by Ex Boyfriend at School After Breakup
item.description:
In October 2023, 21 year old Lilie James was found dead at a school in Sydney, Australia. Authorities confirmed she was killed on campus after being last seen entering a bathroom area with a male colleague.
Investigators identified her former boyfriend, Paul Thijssen, as the suspect. Evidence later showed the attack was planned and carried out shortly after their relationship ended, with findings describing it as a premeditated act.
Police launched a search, but Thijssen was later found dead nearby. Officials concluded the case involved domestic violence, and it prompted national discussion about coercive control and safety in relationships.

media:content.media:title: Student Killed by Ex Boyfriend at School After Breakup
media:content.media:description:
In October 2023, 21 year old Lilie James was found dead at a school in Sydney, Australia. Authorities confirmed she was killed on campus after being last seen entering a bathroom area with a male colleague.
Investigators identified her former boyfriend, Paul Thijssen, as the suspect. Evidence later showed the attack was planned and carried out shortly after their relationship ended, with findings describing it as a premeditated act.
Police launched a search, but Thijssen was later found dead nearby. Officials concluded the case involved domestic violence, and it prompted national discussion about coercive control and safety in relationships.

item.title: Oakley Carlson Vanished at Age 4 and Was Declared Dead in 2025
item.description: Oakley Carlson was last seen in Oakville, Washington on February 10, 2021, at approximately four years old. She was not reported missing until ten months later, when a school principal raised concerns after a welfare check revealed she could not be located. Her biological parents, Jordan Bowers and Andrew Carlson, refused to cooperate with investigators and were later convicted on unrelated child endangerment charges. A state review found child welfare authorities had received 14 reports about the family prior to her disappearance, and a court declared Oakley legally dead in August 2025. Her body has never been found, no murder charges have been filed, and both parents remain suspects as of April 2026.

media:content.media:title: Oakley Carlson Vanished at Age 4 and Was Declared Dead in 2025
media:content.media:description: Oakley Carlson was last seen in Oakville, Washington on February 10, 2021, at approximately four years old. She was not reported missing until ten months later, when a school principal raised concerns after a welfare check revealed she could not be located. Her biological parents, Jordan Bowers and Andrew Carlson, refused to cooperate with investigators and were later convicted on unrelated child endangerment charges. A state review found child welfare authorities had received 14 reports about the family prior to her disappearance, and a court declared Oakley legally dead in August 2025. Her body has never been found, no murder charges have been filed, and both parents remain suspects as of April 2026.

item.title: Rex Heuermann Pleaded Guilty to Killing Eight Women at Gilgo Beach
item.description: In April 2026, Rex Heuermann, 62, pleaded guilty in Suffolk County Court to seven murder charges and admitted to an eighth killing, ending a case that began when remains were discovered along Ocean Parkway near Gilgo Beach in 2010. Heuermann, a former New York architect, admitted to strangling the women and disposing of their remains on Long Island between 1993 and 2010. His sentencing is scheduled for June 17, 2026, where he is expected to receive life in prison without parole.

media:content.media:title: Rex Heuermann Pleaded Guilty to Killing Eight Women at Gilgo Beach
media:content.media:description: In April 2026, Rex Heuermann, 62, pleaded guilty in Suffolk County Court to seven murder charges and admitted to an eighth killing, ending a case that began when remains were discovered along Ocean Parkway near Gilgo Beach in 2010. Heuermann, a former New York architect, admitted to strangling the women and disposing of their remains on Long Island between 1993 and 2010. His sentencing is scheduled for June 17, 2026, where he is expected to receive life in prison without parole.

item.title: Michelle O'Connell: Was the Case Really Closed?
item.description: Michelle O'Connell, 24, was found dead on September 2, 2010, inside the St. Augustine home she shared with her boyfriend, St. Johns County sheriff's deputy Jeremy Banks. Authorities quickly ruled her death a suicide, though her family disputed the finding and the initial investigation faced documented procedural shortcomings. Four separate reviews were conducted over the following years, with investigators reaching conflicting conclusions. The case remains officially classified as a suicide. No charges have ever been filed and Jeremy Banks continued serving as a deputy as of the most recent available reports.

media:content.media:title: Michelle O'Connell: Was the Case Really Closed?
media:content.media:description: Michelle O'Connell, 24, was found dead on September 2, 2010, inside the St. Augustine home she shared with her boyfriend, St. Johns County sheriff's deputy Jeremy Banks. Authorities quickly ruled her death a suicide, though her family disputed the finding and the initial investigation faced documented procedural shortcomings. Four separate reviews were conducted over the following years, with investigators reaching conflicting conclusions. The case remains officially classified as a suicide. No charges have ever been filed and Jeremy Banks continued serving as a deputy as of the most recent available reports.
